milestone_brief: Close the deferred F-M2 + F-L1 + F-L2 findings from status-bar-bbox-2026q2-e1: switch the status-bar spatial extent readout from the half-extent ±max format ('bbox ±a × ±b × ±c') to a full-extent format aligned with peer scientific-viz tools. Concretely: the previous milestone's frontend-ux critic flagged that ParaView, MeshLab, and Blender all use full-extent widths (size/dimensions/X Range labels) and NONE use the ± half-extent convention; that AVC's '±max' is a silent over-approximation for the 3 Hanson parametric generators (theta sweeps [0, π/2], non-symmetric bounds); and that .2f precision creates false equalities at sub-1.0 extents. This milestone replaces the readout. Proposed format from the previous deferral: 'size: Lx × Ly × Lz' where Lx = mesh.bounds[1] - mesh.bounds[0] (true full extent), with .3f precision. The full-extent format eliminates the Hanson over-approximation honestly: a Hanson surface that's -1.07..+1.19 in x reads as 'size: 2.26' (the actual diameter), no ± misleading prefix. Additionally the previous v1 deferred F-M1 (status-bar overflow on Dwork warning path) was already addressed in the v1 rect via priority reordering — that work stays. AI invariants: AI-9 (no processEvents added — string format change only), AI-10 (read still uses self._raw_mesh inside success branch, no mesh regen, AI-14 unchanged — error paths set _raw_mesh=None and return before format line). Test surface: tests/test_status_bar_bbox.py — update BBOX_FORMAT and BBOX_REGEX to match the new 'size: Lx × Ly × Lz' format with .3f precision; the Hanson regression test no longer needs the math.isfinite over-approximation guard since the new format reports actual extent (still keep the isfinite check though — guards against NaN-producing future generator changes regardless of format). CONTEXT.md §4.3 status-bar bbox paragraph: rewrite to reflect the new format and remove the 'honest over-approximation' caveat (no longer needed); the warning-path priority note from v1 stays. Roadmap acceptance: peer-vocabulary alignment (ParaView/MeshLab/Blender), Hanson honesty, .3f precision avoids false equalities at sub-1.0 extents. Out of scope: hover-coord readout (separate v2 deferral; defer to dedicated milestone with mouse-position-tracking infrastructure).
research_synthesis: Switch app.py:_render_current bbox token from 'bbox ±max × ±max × ±max' (.2f) to 'size: Lx × Ly × Lz' (.3f) using bounds[1]-bounds[0] etc — true full extent honest for all 14 generators including Hanson. Rename bbox_suffix → size_suffix (3 lines). BBOX_REGEX must use \d{3} (not \d+) to enforce .3f. Hanson isfinite guard extends to all 6 bounds indices (full-extent subtracts b[0]/b[2]/b[4] too). Remove 'honest over-approximation' framing from CONTEXT.md §4.3; warning-path priority note stays.
